page: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by a 307 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile characterized by moderate single-core capabilities but constrained multi-core scaling. The single-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1898.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1648.0. However, multi-core performance falls into the weak category, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3869.36 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3539.0. Memory bandwidth is also asymmetrical; while large block write bandwidth is average at 25.35 GB/sec, read-only bandwidth is weak, measuring 30.58 GB/sec for large blocks.

The primary tradeoff of the E8-4ds_v5 is its high memory-to-vCPU ratio paired with relatively modest computational throughput. This makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. It is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. Conversely, it is less optimal for compute-heavy tasks like software compilation or multi-threaded decompression, where benchmarks like clang compilation and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scored in the weak and poor ranges, respectively.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 307 GB local disk. While single-core CPU benchmarks and memory write bandwidth perform at average levels, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. The server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads such as databases and caching systems that do not demand heavy parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 307 GB local disk, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
